justifications.reason: Vite production bundle committed for operational serving of the bindings docs site without a node toolchain; machine-generated, not hand-maintained source.
justifications.mitigation: Regenerated wholesale by the docs build; hand edits prohibited. Module boundaries live in the TypeScript sources it is bundled from.

justifications.reason: Emscripten-emitted WASM loader glue for the browser demo, committed for operational serving; machine-generated output of the emscripten CMake build.
justifications.mitigation: Regenerated by build:wasm; never edited by hand. The module boundary is enforced at the C TU level and the exports.txt whitelist that produce it.

justifications.reason: Emscripten-emitted WASM loader glue copied into the docs site for operational serving; machine-generated, not hand-maintained.
justifications.mitigation: Regenerated by build:wasm and copied by the docs pipeline; the maintained module boundary is the emscripten build, not this artifact.

justifications.reason: pnpm lockfile; machine-generated dependency resolution record kept for operational build reproducibility of the JS packages.
justifications.mitigation: Maintained exclusively by pnpm install; never split or hand-edited.

justifications.reason: Cohesive two-site DMRG driver: the Lanczos local solver, zgemm environment contraction, and sweep control share hot per-sweep workspace state kept static-scoped in one TU for cross-sweep inlining.
justifications.mitigation: v1.2 arc: extract the environment contractor (dmrg_env.c) and the Lanczos local eigensolver (dmrg_lanczos.c) behind internal headers, leaving dmrg.c as sweep orchestration.

justifications.reason: Cohesive dense-tensor kernel registry (contraction, QR, SVD, LAPACK dispatch plus no-LAPACK fallbacks) consumed by every tensor-network module; the LAPACK/fallback pairing is verified as one unit under QSIM_FORCE_*_FALLBACK.
justifications.mitigation: v1.2 arc: split decomposition kernels (tensor_decomp.c: QR/SVD + fallbacks) from contraction/layout kernels, keeping the public tensor.h boundary unchanged.

justifications.reason: Cohesive VQE solver: Hamiltonians, ansaetze, optimizers, energy, and the adjoint/parameter-shift gradient share solver-internal structs deliberately not exposed in vqe.h; a single TU keeps that non-ABI surface private.
justifications.mitigation: v1.2 arc: extract optimizer implementations (vqe_optim.c) and the diff-circuit gradient bridge (vqe_grad.c) behind an internal header once the stable-ABI gradient wrapper (task vqe-gradient-stable-abi) lands.

justifications.reason: Cohesive self-contained WebGPU backend: device setup, WGSL kernel sources, and dispatch are co-located because the backend must compile standalone under emscripten.
justifications.mitigation: v1.2 arc: hoist WGSL kernel strings into a generated header and split pipeline setup from dispatch once the WebGPU path gains C-side tests.

justifications.reason: Cohesive anyon module: fusion-tree algebra and the surface-code scaffold share tableau and fusion-space internals kept static in one TU.
justifications.mitigation: v1.2 arc: split anyon/fusion-tree algebra from the surface-code module along the existing header section boundaries.

justifications.reason: Cohesive connection state machine: POSIX TCP transport, TLS/mTLS, HMAC auth, token bucket, and metrics share one wire-format implementation, with the TLS path ifdef-gated inside the same TU by design (documented in CMakeLists).
justifications.mitigation: v1.2 arc: extract rate limiting + metrics (control_plane_guard.c) and TLS session plumbing (control_plane_tls.c) behind internal headers, keeping the wire format in one module.

justifications.reason: Cohesive MPS measurement kernel set: sampling and expectation share contraction caches and the GPU-backend dispatch seam; splitting now would duplicate the cache plumbing.
justifications.mitigation: v1.2 arc: extract the sampling paths from expectation/observable evaluation into a separate module once the GPU dispatch seam stabilizes.

justifications.reason: Cohesive many-body-localisation study pipeline: Hamiltonian build, exact diagonalisation, and level-statistics analysis are consumed as a unit by the MBL examples.
justifications.mitigation: v1.2 arc: split the ED core from statistics/observables into separate modules when the MBL surface is promoted beyond research status.
